profile.behavior: Primarily a foliage gleaner; uses its stout bill for seeds, fruit, and insects, and may hover or fly out to catch insects.
field_marks: Stocky, broad-chested songbird with a short neck and medium-length squared tail.
Very large, thick triangular bill, pale horn-colored in the adult male.
Adult male black above with crisp white wing patches and white underparts.
Adult male has a vivid rose-red breast chevron below the black throat.
White patches show in the wings and tail; males show pink-red underwings in flight.
Female and immature are brown and heavily streaked, with a bold pale eyebrow and pale bill.
profile.habitat: Moist deciduous or mixed woodland, especially second-growth woods, thickets, and shrubby forest edges; also uses semiopen wooded habitats.

sources.title: Rose-breasted Grosbeak Identification — All About Birds
Rose-breasted Grosbeak Life History — All About Birds

findings: Verified as an adult male Rose-breasted Grosbeak: correct black head/back, pale heavy triangular bill, white underparts and wing/tail patches, and rose-red breast chevron. The illustration has one head, one beak, two legs, and one tail; the far wing is naturally occluded, while the lower wing is a separate anatomical study.

All visible text is factually consistent with the verification sources, including family, scientific name, behavior, habitat, and measurements. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

Minor visual stylization: the breast patch is broader and more vertically extended than in the supplied photos, and the tail is rendered a little less square-ended; neither is a material species-identification error.
